---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
---



```{r}
# SCRIPT 2/2 - USe manually indicated X chromosome to cut out the single molecule localizations within it:
# Input: your path to the directory containing output from SCRIPT1 and full FOV localization files from Stormtracker
# Output:
# Next: perform fast tracking or slow tracking using output file with localizations
#
# uses: https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/ptinpoly/ptinpoly.pdf
library(ptinpoly); library(stringr);
##################################################################
data<-"D:/SPT/27.09.2022/27.09.2022_B12_day 0_DOX24h_tracking_Xi composite and pos.out_Random region"   # <<<<< indicate your path!
##################################################################




ROI.names <- list.files(data, full.names = TRUE, recursive = FALSE, pattern = "Random.csv") # ".csv" for .csv files only 
file.names <- list.files(data, full.names = TRUE, recursive = FALSE, pattern = "pos.out") #

for (i in 1:length(file.names)){ # % % % % 
### load localizations:
df<-read.table(file.names[i], colClasses = "factor")
df2<-as.data.frame.matrix(df)
colnames(df2)<-df2[1,]; 
df2<-df2[-1,];  # now df2 is a regular data.frame that can be then filtered
### load ROI of X-chromosome ###
polygon<-read.csv(ROI.names[i])

#Queries<-data.frame(cbind(df2$XCENTER, df2$YCENTER)); colnames(Vertices)<-c("X", "Y")
#Vertices<- data.frame(cbind(polygon$X, polygon$Y))

Queries<-cbind(df2$XCENTER, df2$YCENTER); #colnames(Vertices)<-c("X", "Y")
Vertices <- cbind(polygon$X, polygon$Y)
  # Test if the localization lies within the X chromosome (Polygon), -1 is NO, 1 is YES:
  df3<- pip2d(Vertices,Queries)  

localizations.to.filter<-data.frame(df[-1,], df3)
localizations.to.filter2 <- subset(localizations.to.filter, df3 == 1)
localizations.to.filter3 <- localizations.to.filter2[1:(length(localizations.to.filter2)-1)]  # remove last column
colnames(localizations.to.filter3) <- c("FRAME", "XCENTER", "YCENTER", "BRIGHTNESS", "BG","I0","S_X", "S_Y", "THETA", "ECC")
          
name.split<-str_split(file.names[i], pattern="/")
len<-length(str_split(file.names[i], pattern="/")[[1]])
base.name<-str_split(file.names[i], pattern="/")[[1]][len]


if (nrow(localizations.to.filter3)>0){ # $$$$   Aleks added it on 6.05.2022, empty X-chromosomes wont be saved


# now save it in the same format as it is for tracking!
#Option 1 - worked:
write.table(localizations.to.filter3, file = paste(data, "/X_chromosome_",base.name, sep=""), quote=FALSE, append = FALSE, sep = " ", dec = ".",
            row.names = FALSE, col.names = TRUE)   # row.names=FALSE ensures that no index is included to the saved data
             # quote=FALSE prevents from adding "" in every cell

# Option 2 - not worked:
#write.table(localizations.to.filter3,paste(data, "/X_chromosome_",base.name, sep=""),sep="\t",row.names=FALSE)

} # $$$$

}# % % % % 



```
